                            Reason for unemployment

                               ^ Data r e l a t e to production workers in mining and manufacturing: to construction workers in contract construction: and to nonsupervisory workers in transportation and public
                          utilities; wholesale and r e t a i l t r a d e ; finance, insurance, and real estate; and services. These groups account for approximately four-fifths of the total employment on private
                          nonagricultural payrolls.
                             ^Beginning January 1965, data relate to railroads with operating revenues of $5,000,000 ot more.
                             ^Daca for nonsupervisory workers exclude messengers.
                             ^Data for nonoffice salesmen excluded from nonsupervisory count for all series in this division.

                               Prepared by the U.S. Civil Service Commission. Data relate to civilian employment only and exclude Central Intelligence and National Security Agencies.

                     SEASONALLY ADJUSTED EMPLOYMENT
                                                  T a b l e B-4:      I n d e x e s of e m p l o y m e n t on n o n a g r i c u l t u r a l p a y r o l l s , by industry division,

                             S u b a r e a o f W a s h i n g t o n , D. C. S t a n d a r d M e t r o p o l i t a n S t a t i s t i c a l A r e a .

                          p = preliminary.

                                                                     Seasonal                Adjustments

                            Many economic statistics reflect a regularly recurring sea-       approximation based on past experience. Seasonally adjusted
                      sonal movement which can be estimated on the basis of past              estimates have a broader         margin of possible error than the
                      experience. By eliminating that part of the change which can             original data on which they are based, since they are subject
                      be ascribed to usual seasonal variation, it is possible to ob-           not only to sampling and other errors but, in addition, are
                      serve the cyclical and other nonseasonal movements in the                affected by the uncertainties of the seasonal adjustment proc-
                      series. However, in evaluating deviations from the seasonal              ess itself. Seasonally adjusted series for selected labor force
                      p a t t e r n — t h a t is, changes in a seasonally adjusted series—     and establishment data are published regularly in           Employment
                      it is important to note that seasonal adjustment is merely an            and Earnings.

                      Gross average w e e k l y earnings                        P r o d u c t of gross average w e e k l y hours and                         P r o d u c t of gross average w e e k l y hours and
                                                                                      average h o u r l y earnings.                                                average h o u r l y earnings.

                      Labor t u r n o v e r rates                               Sum of m o n t h l y rates divided by 12.                                    S u m of m o n t h l y rates d i v i d e d by 12.

                      Job vacancy rates                                         S u m of m o n t h l y rates d i v i d e d by 12.                            S u m o f m o n t h l y rates d i v i d e d b y 12.
